title: Artist & Label Services Companies Are Multiplying Rapidly. How Do You Know They’re Legit? (Guest Column)
summary: When it comes to choosing professionals to support their careers, independent artists are spoiled for choice. Here’s what they should look for.
body: Have you ever noticed that music industry folks tend to change their occupation to “consultant” on their LinkedIn profile after getting laid off? For years, being a consultant meant you were trying to make a little money while you were between jobs. Today, however, people who were unlucky enough to lose their jobs are becoming full-time consultants rather than going back to corporate work.   The music industry is witnessing an explosion of seasoned executives from major and indie record labels who are establishing their own boutique artist and label services firms. Most of them were laid off after spending many years learning how to market music, building important relationships and generally understanding how to create a smooth, organized process to maximize a music release’s potential.  Related Artists Want Help Breaking Through the Noise — Just Not From Major Labels, Survey Finds New Levels Strike Global Partnership With Virgin Music Group, Signs Hooligan Hefs & More Bad Bunny's Australia Debut Sets Attendance Benchmark in Sydney There’s plenty of demand for their servic…
